text.title: FastAPI Error Handler Implementation
text.summary: ProblemDetail Pydantic model, FastAPI exception handler wiring for validation and generic errors, and logging requirements.

section.title: ProblemDetail Model

rule.statement: Define a single ProblemDetail Pydantic model shared across all exception handlers. Never construct error dicts inline in handlers.
rule.rationale: A shared model enforces schema consistency across all error paths and enables OpenAPI schema generation for the error response type.

section.reference: ProblemDetail model and FastAPI handler wiring

example.code:
from pydantic import BaseModel
class ProblemDetail(BaseModel): type: str title: str status: int detail: str | None = None instance: str | None = None trace_id: str | None = None errors: list[dict] | None = None

rule.statement: Register at minimum two exception handlers: one for Pydantic ValidationError that maps field errors to the errors array, and one catch-all for Exception that logs the full traceback and returns a generic 500 without internal details.
rule.rationale: The two-handler pattern ensures validation errors produce actionable field-level messages while unexpected errors are logged server-side but sanitised for the client.

antipattern.description: Returning exc.args[0] or str(exc) in the 500 detail field.
antipattern.reason: Leaks internal implementation details (file paths, SQL queries, variable names) to untrusted clients.
